# v13: close the silent session-summary gap with a provenance-labeled degraded fallback (Option F)

## Problem (v13 release-blocker)

`SessionService.summarizeSession` joins raw turn documents and one-shots them; a guardrail skips invocation when the prompt exceeds the local-model safe-processing band (`localModels.chat.safeProcessingLimitTokens`) and returns `null`. So an oversized night-shift session (200+ turns, > context) gets **no session summary at all** — a silent gap, not merely a lossy one.

## Root cause (V-B-A'd)

- `ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.mjs` — `summarizeSession` joins raw documents (~`:503`) + the guardrail returns null past the safe band (~`:593`).
- The per-turn `miniSummary` projection already exists (`MemoryService._hydrateRecentTurnSummaries` + the #12746 / #12802 / #12805 backfill), ~280 char/turn → ~15k tokens for 200 turns (one-shots), but `summarizeSession` ignores it.

## Fix — Option F (graduated from #12817 LEAF 1; gpt `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]`)

- Within the guardrail: **raw one-shot, unchanged** (preferred path).
- When the raw prompt would exceed the safe band: instead of returning null, build the session summary from the per-turn miniSummaries and tag it with provenance — `sourceTier: 'miniSummary'`, `degraded: true`, `rawCanonical: true`. Raw turns stay the canonical drill-down substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An oversized session (raw prompt > `safeProcessingLimitTokens`) produces a **non-null** session summary built from per-turn miniSummaries (no silent skip).
- [ ] The fallback summary carries explicit provenance metadata (`sourceTier` / `degraded` / `rawCanonical`) so consumers distinguish it from a raw-grounded summary.
- [ ] The raw path is unchanged when within the guardrail (no regression).
- [ ] Graph extraction (`DreamService` / `SemanticGraphExtractor`) continues to consume **raw** turns, never the degraded index (V-B-A confirmed it reads raw today — preserve).
- [ ] Deterministic unit coverage: within-band → raw summary; over-band → miniSummary-derived labeled fallback (not null).

## Out of scope

- The fidelity **eval** + the A/B/C/E architecture choice (#12817 LEAF 2, parked post-v13; eval bar = false-arc + pivotal-signal preservation, not raw detail-retention).
- Promoting miniSummary-derived from fallback to *primary* (the LEAF 2 research question, per @tobiu's premise-reframe).

## Provenance / relations

Graduated from Ideation Discussion #12817 (LEAF 1). Relates to #12679 (temporal pyramid), #12065 (REM-SSOT epic), #12073 (chunker), #12799 (sibling silent-degradation family).

Authored by Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code)

Addresses the cross-family review: the degraded fallback only entered when stored
miniSummaries existed, so an oversized session whose turns had no miniSummary (the fail-soft
buildMiniSummary / backfill case) still fell through to return null — the exact silent-gap
class #12819 closes.

- summarizeSession fallback now builds per-turn input the _hydrateRecentTurnSummaries way:
  stored miniSummary when present, else a truncated raw snippet (280 chars). It never falls
  through to a null summary when raw turns exist. sourceTier labels the actual source
  ('miniSummary' when any stored gist was used, else 'truncatedRaw'); degraded + rawCanonical
  unchanged.
- getSessionMiniSummaries now returns a memory-id -> miniSummary Map so the fallback joins
  per turn (the graph node id == the Chroma document id).
- new deterministic test: over-band raw + zero stored miniSummaries -> non-null truncated-raw
  degraded summary. 4/4 green."

- 2026-06-09T11:55:26Z @tobiu referenced in commit `bacec80` - "fix(memory-core): close the silent session-summary gap with a degraded miniSummary fallback (#12819) (#12820)

* fix(memory-core): close the silent session-summary gap with a degraded miniSummary fallback (#12819)

SessionService.summarizeSession joined raw turn documents and returned null when the
guardrail pre-check found the prompt exceeded the local model's safe processing band, so an
oversized night-shift session (200+ turns) got NO session summary at all (a silent gap).

- summarizeSession: extracted a buildSummaryPrompt(content) + runGuardrailed(prompt, note)
  closure. The raw path is unchanged (preferred). When the raw prompt is size-precheck-skipped,
  fall back to a summary built from the per-turn miniSummaries (a far smaller, already-compact
  input that one-shots) instead of returning null. The persisted summary is provenance-labeled
  (sourceTier:'raw'|'miniSummary', degraded, rawCanonical:true) on both the Chroma summary
  metadata and the graph SESSION_SUMMARY node. Raw turns stay canonical; graph extraction
  continues to consume raw, never the degraded index.
- new getSessionMiniSummaries(sessionId): graph-scoped, chronological per-turn miniSummaries
  (miniSummary lives in the AGENT_MEMORY node's data.properties, not Chroma metadata).
- new deterministic spec (CI-safe: in-memory graph, mocked model + collections):
  getSessionMiniSummaries ordering/filter; over-band -> degraded fallback; within-band -> raw.

Graduated from the session-summary fidelity ideation, LEAF 1 (cross-family GRADUATION_APPROVED).
The fidelity research (A/B/C/E + eval) stays parked as LEAF 2, post-v13.

* fix(memory-core): cover the no-miniSummary fallback branch with truncated-raw (#12819)

Addresses the cross-family review: the degraded fallback only entered when stored
miniSummaries existed, so an oversized session whose turns had no miniSummary (the fail-soft
buildMiniSummary / backfill case) still fell through to return null — the exact silent-gap
class #12819 closes.

- summarizeSession fallback now builds per-turn input the _hydrateRecentTurnSummaries way:
  stored miniSummary when present, else a truncated raw snippet (280 chars). It never falls
  through to a null summary when raw turns exist. sourceTier labels the actual source
  ('miniSummary' when any stored gist was used, else 'truncatedRaw'); degraded + rawCanonical
  unchanged.
- getSessionMiniSummaries now returns a memory-id -> miniSummary Map so the fallback joins
  per turn (the graph node id == the Chroma document id).
- new deterministic test: over-band raw + zero stored miniSummaries -> non-null truncated-raw
  degraded summary. 4/4 green."
- 2026-06-09T11:55:26Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-06-09T11:58:46Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `dd868a1` - "feat(orchestrator): log the session-summary backlog count on the periodic sweep (#12821)

The mini-summary backfill task only fires when there is a backlog, so it always logs
pending-memory-minisummary:N (#12810). The summary task also fires on the periodic sweep, whose
reason was bare periodic-sweep:600000 — so the session-summary backlog depth was invisible on
its dominant path.

- new getPendingSessionSummaryCount(db): fail-soft graph-direct count of sessions with
  AGENT_MEMORY turns but no SESSION_SUMMARY (the real unsummarized-session backlog, not the
  capped SummarizationJobs marker fetch).
- buildSummaryTrigger appends pending-session-summary:N to the periodic-sweep reason when the
  count is available; reason otherwise unchanged (backward-compatible).
- getDueTask computes the count only when the periodic sweep will fire (no handover, no marked
  jobs, interval due) so the COUNT query runs at most once per sweep, never per poll.
- +4 deterministic scheduling tests; existing reason shape preserved when no count is provided.

Parallel to #12810 (mini-summary backlog count). Relates to #12819 / #12820, #12817 LEAF 1."
- 2026-06-09T12:35:55Z @tobiu referenced in commit `6a6b0bf` - "feat(orchestrator): log the session-summary backlog count on the periodic sweep (#12821) (#12822)

* feat(orchestrator): log the session-summary backlog count on the periodic sweep (#12821)

The mini-summary backfill task only fires when there is a backlog, so it always logs
pending-memory-minisummary:N (#12810). The summary task also fires on the periodic sweep, whose
reason was bare periodic-sweep:600000 — so the session-summary backlog depth was invisible on
its dominant path.

- new getPendingSessionSummaryCount(db): fail-soft graph-direct count of sessions with
  AGENT_MEMORY turns but no SESSION_SUMMARY (the real unsummarized-session backlog, not the
  capped SummarizationJobs marker fetch).
- buildSummaryTrigger appends pending-session-summary:N to the periodic-sweep reason when the
  count is available; reason otherwise unchanged (backward-compatible).
- getDueTask computes the count only when the periodic sweep will fire (no handover, no marked
  jobs, interval due) so the COUNT query runs at most once per sweep, never per poll.
- +4 deterministic scheduling tests; existing reason shape preserved when no count is provided.

Parallel to #12810 (mini-summary backlog count). Relates to #12819 / #12820, #12817 LEAF 1.

* fix(orchestrator): null-safe anti-join in getPendingSessionSummaryCount (#12821)

Addresses the cross-family review: the unsummarized-session count used NOT IN (subquery), which
is not null-safe — a single SESSION_SUMMARY row with a NULL sessionId makes NOT IN evaluate to
NULL for every row, silently collapsing the backlog count to 0. Switched to a correlated
NOT EXISTS, which is immune to the NULL trap.

- new real-graph spec (SessionSummaryBacklogCount.spec.mjs, :memory: under UNIT_TEST_MODE,
  CI-safe — no Chroma/live model): proves the count reports unsummarized sessions and that a
  NULL-sessionId SESSION_SUMMARY does not collapse it; plus fail-soft.

18/18 scheduling specs green."
